item.title: Game Hacking: Using Dynamic Patching to Modify Games
item.description: Introduction Have you ever considered modifying the source code of a game as you wish and playing with that modification? In this text, we will demonstrate how to modify a game in real-time using low-level knowledge, specifically assembly language. Additionally, we will develop a simple Python program to automate the process for us, employing the memory patching technique. Before proceeding, it is entirely possible that some of the expressions mentioned above may not be familiar to you.

item.title: Hyperbleed - Current state of spectre-BTI mitigations on cloud
item.description: Version 1.0 Introduction During our tests for reverse spectre attacks 123, we have observed that the behavior of the spectre-BTI4 mitigations differs between a bare-metal and a cloud-based scenario. The Linux kernel allows userspace processes to enable mitigations by calling prctl5 with the PR_GET_SPECULATION_CTRL which disables the speculation feature or by using seccomp6. The default behavior changed over time (from using IBPB/STIBP to IBRS). We have measured that on some instances of Google, AWS, Azure and Oracle, the spectre-BTI mitigation using prctl still leaves the victim exposed to attacks in some cases.

item.title: About GRIS
item.description: We work in the Computer Science Department of the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro and in other requesting units of the University. Our objective is the detection, resolution and prevention of security incidents, in addition to offering academic support to UFRJ students who have a particular interest in the area of ​​information security. Among the main activities of GRIS are: Apply security updates Record and monitor security incidents Evaluate network security conditions Disseminate security practices and recommendations Assist in repairing damage caused by security incidents Analyze compromised systems looking for causes, damages and culprits (forensic analysis) Provide education and training for system administrators The main mission of GRIS, as a project of the Department of Computer Science, is to offer guidance and academic support to students of the Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science at the Department of Computer Science at UFRJ, a non-profit organization, with regard to the theme of Information Security in all its aspects, in the areas of teaching, research and extension, and this is the main, if not the only, guiding criterion for internal and external issues.
